file.content:
# Kapow Data Import Tool ‚Äî Design Document

## Context

Kapow (College Power Search) has a fully defined PostgreSQL schema and a working
API/frontend, but no data import pipeline. The database needs to be populated
from government data (NCES College Scorecard, Clery Act crime stats), college
ranking publications (US News, Forbes, Niche, etc.), athletic conference data
(NCAA), climate data (Koppen), and other sources. This tool provides a single Go
binary with subcommands to import all data sources, with download+cache support
and proper documentation for sources that require web scraping.

## Architecture

### Module: `tools/importer/` (standalone Go module, like `tools/samplesql/`)

```
tools/importer/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ go.mod                       # github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ main.go                      # CLI entrypoint: subcommand dispatch
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ registry.go                  # Importer registration table + run-all orchestrator
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ DATA_SOURCES.md              # Data source URLs, scraping instructions, update cadence
‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ internal/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ config/config.go         # DATABASE_URL, CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ dbutil/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ connect.go           # pgxpool connection with retry
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ upsert.go            # UpsertInstitution, UpsertDataset, UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ batch.go             # CopyFrom bulk insert, BatchExec
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ views.go             # RefreshMaterializedViews
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fetch/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fetch.go             # HTTP download + ETag cache + decompression + local fallback
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ fetch_test.go
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ csvutil/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ reader.go            # Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader with typed column access
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ reader_test.go
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ namematch/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ matcher.go           # Multi-tier institution name matching
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ normalize.go         # Name canonicalization rules
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ aliases.go           # Hardcoded alias overrides (~1000 institution name mappings)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fuzzy.go             # Tier 2: Jaro-Winkler fuzzy matching (adrg/strutil)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ llm.go               # Tier 3: LLM-assisted curation (Claude API, optional)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ matcher_test.go
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ranking/
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ builder.go           # Opinion type, RankingBuilder: accumulate ‚Üí batch flush
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ builder_test.go
‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nces/                        # NCES College Scorecard (institution foundation)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nces.go
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fields.go                # CSV column ‚Üí JSONB key mapping (200+ fields)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ nces_test.go
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ncesfos/                     # NCES Field of Study (CIP codes)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ rankings/                    # All 9+ ranking publications (one file each)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ usnews.go, forbes.go, niche.go, timeshighered.go,
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   qs.go, washmonthly.go, wsj.go, arwu.go, money.go
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ rankings_test.go
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ncaa/                        # NCAA athletics
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ncaa.go
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sports.go                # Sport‚Üígender/category mapping
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ weather/                     # Koppen climate zones
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ crime/                       # Clery Act crime stats
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ admissions/                  # Application platforms (Common App, Coalition, etc.)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ platforms.go             # Embedded platform membership lists
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ misc/                        # Partisan lean, AAU, metro area
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ zipcoords/                   # ZIP code coordinates
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ normalizer/                  # institution_normalized_alias generator
```

### CLI Design

Single binary, subcommand dispatch via stdlib `flag`:

```
kapow-import <command> [flags]

Commands:
  nces             Import NCES College Scorecard institutions
  nces-fos         Import NCES Field of Study data
  rankings         Import rankings (--publication=usnews|forbes|niche|...)
  ncaa             Import NCAA athletics data
  weather          Import Koppen climate classification
  crime            Import Clery Act crime statistics
  admissions       Import application platform membership
  misc             Import misc data (partisan lean, AAU, metro area)
  zipcoords        Import ZIP code coordinates
  normalizer       Generate normalized institution aliases
  run-all          Run all importers in dependency order
  match-review     Review unmatched names from last run (fuzzy + LLM suggestions)
  sources          Print data source documentation

Global flags:
  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow-import)
  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB

Per-command flags:
  --file PATH      Use local file instead of downloading
  --year YYYY      Data year (where applicable)
  rankings --publication SLUG [--category SLUG]
  run-all --skip=weather,metro  |  --only=nces,rankings
```

### Importer Interface

```go
type Importer interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Dependencies() []string
    RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet)
    Run(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config, pool *pgxpool.Pool, fetcher *fetch.Fetcher) error
}
```

### Shared Infrastructure

**`internal/fetch`** ‚Äî Download + cache. HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
conditional requests. Cache keyed by URL hash. Transparent .zip/.gz/.xz
decompression. `--file` flag bypasses download. Stale cache preferred over
failure when source is temporarily unavailable.

**`internal/csvutil`** ‚Äî Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader. `Row.String("COL")`,
`Row.Int("COL")`, `Row.OptionalFloat("COL")` typed accessors. Avoids
repetitive index-based column access.

**`internal/namematch`** ‚Äî Multi-tier institution name resolution:

- **Tier 1 ‚Äî Deterministic** (free, instant, ~95% of matches):
  Loads all institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. Exact name ‚Üí
  normalized name ‚Üí alias lookup. Also supports OPE6/OPE8 ID lookups.
  Normalization: lowercase, strip diacritics, remove "The ", strip punctuation,
  collapse whitespace. Includes ~1000 hardcoded alias overrides for institutions
  whose external names differ from their NCES canonical names.

- **Tier 2 ‚Äî Fuzzy string similarity** (free, fast, ~3-4% more):
  Uses [adrg/strutil](https://github.com/adrg/strutil) for Jaro-Winkler
  similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all institutions
  (pre-filtered by state when available). Matches above threshold (0.92) are
  flagged as candidates. Returned with confidence scores for human review.

- **Tier 3 ‚Äî LLM-assisted curation** (optional, pay-per-use, long tail):
  For names that Tier 1+2 can't resolve. Batches unmatched names with context
  (source publication, state, ranking position, top fuzzy candidates) and sends
  to Claude API for suggested matches. Suggestions presented for human approval.

  All approved matches (from Tier 2 or 3) are persisted as new aliases in
  `search_base.institution_alias` for future deterministic matching.

- **`match-review` subcommand**: Interactive review of unmatched names from the
  last import run. Shows fuzzy candidates + LLM suggestions. `--approve` saves
  selected matches as aliases.

**`internal/ranking`** ‚Äî `Opinion` type (rank/score/tier/range methods).
`RankingBuilder` accumulates opinions and batch-flushes to `search_base.ranking`
metadata + `search_base.annual_dataset` + `institution.rankings` JSONB. All 9
ranking importers use this; their own code is just parse + match + derive opinion.

**`internal/dbutil`** ‚Äî Connection pool with retry. `UpsertInstitution` (core
columns + JSONB merge). `MergeJSONB` (append to institution JSONB column without
overwriting other importers' data). `UpsertDataset` / `UpsertAnnualDataset`.
`RefreshMaterializedViews` (called once at end of run-all).

### Pipeline Ordering (`run-all`)

```
Layer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
Layer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
                       admissions, misc/*
Layer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
Final:                RefreshMaterializedViews
```

## Data Sources

### Data Update Calendar

Each source has an "expected" date (when it typically drops) and a "by" date
(99.5% confidence it's out, without being a full year later).

| Source | Expected Release | By Date | Notes |
|--------|-----------------|---------|-------|
| **College Scorecard** | Late April‚ÄìMay | Jun 30 | Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024. Oct update adds FSA data. |
| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same as above | Jun 30 | Bundled with institution data release |
| **US News** | Late Sep | Oct 15 | Sep 23 2025, Sep 2024. Always September. |
| **Forbes** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 26 2025, Sep 7 2024. Late Aug‚Äìearly Sep. |
| **Niche** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 25 2025. Consistently late August. |
| **Times Higher Ed** | Early‚Äìmid Oct | Nov 1 | Oct 9 2025, Oct 10 2024. Always October. |
| **QS World** | Mid Jun | Jul 15 | Jun 19 2025, Jun 4 2024. Always June. |
| **Washington Monthly** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 24 2025. Published with Sep/Oct issue. |
| **WSJ** | Early Sep | Oct 1 | Sep 5 2024. Fall release. |
| **ARWU/Shanghai** | Mid Aug | Sep 1 | Aug 15 2025. Consistently mid-August since 2003. |
| **Money** | Late Jun | Jul 31 | Jun 25 2025. Summer release. |
| **Crime/Clery** | Oct 1 deadline | Nov 15 | Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
| **NCAA Directory** | Aug (academic year) | Sep 30 | Updated for each academic year. |
| **Partisan Lean** | Post-election | Mar (odd years) | FiveThirtyEight updates after each election cycle. |
| **ZIP Coordinates** | Stable | N/A | GeoNames data; updated continuously but rarely changes. |
| **Koppen Climate** | Stable | N/A | Climate classifications; updated every ~10 years. |

**Suggested annual import schedule:**
1. **May‚ÄìJun**: College Scorecard + FOS (foundation refresh), QS, Money
2. **Aug‚ÄìSep**: ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ
3. **Oct‚ÄìNov**: Times Higher Ed, Crime/Clery
4. **After each run**: normalizer, materialized view refresh

### Programmatic Download (automatable)

| Source | URL / API | Format |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| **College Scorecard** | `https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/` ‚Äî bulk CSV download | CSV (ZIP) |
| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same site, FieldOfStudyData files | CSV (ZIP) |
| **Forbes Rankings** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=...` | JSON |
| **Niche Rankings** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college` (109 pages, needs User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests) | JSON |
| **Crime Data** | `https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list` ‚Äî downloadable XLS, convert to CSV via libreoffice | XLS‚ÜíCSV |
| **Partisan Lean** | FiveThirtyEight CSV (GitHub) | CSV |
| **ZIP Coordinates** | Public domain CSV (GeoNames or similar) | CSV |
| **Koppen Climate** | Public domain ASCII grid | Text |
| **PayScale** | `https://www.payscale.com/college-roi/page/2` ‚Äî extract from page props JSON | HTML‚ÜíJSON |

### Web Scraping Required (semi-automated with docs)

| Source | Website | Method | Notes |
|--------|---------|--------|-------|
| **ARWU/Shanghai** | `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` | Scrape HTML table or download PDF | No API. PDF at `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`. Use goquery for HTML or manual PDF download. |
| **NCAA Sports** | `web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` | Scrape or manual export | NCAA Directory has member listings by division. May need browser automation or manual CSV export. |
| **Money Magazine** | `money.com/best-colleges/` | Scrape HTML table | Extract `tr[data-id]` rows, parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` via goquery |
| **Princeton Review** | princetonreview.com | Scrape HTML | Category-based lists (most liberal, most conservative, etc.) |
| **Times Higher Ed** | `timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` | JSON API behind the page, or scrape | May need browser dev tools to find the API endpoint |
| **QS World** | QS website | Previously available as TSV/CSV | Check if still downloadable or needs scraping |
| **Washington Monthly** | washingtonmonthly.com | JSON or scrape | Check current availability |
| **WSJ Rankings** | WSJ website | JSON embedded in page | `{ colleges: [...] }` structure |

### Pre-curated / Aggregated Sources

| Source | Location | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------|
| **US News Historical** | [frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings](https://github.com/frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings) | CSV archive 1984‚Äì2024. Free for public use. |
| **Multi-ranking aggregate** | [frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data](https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data) | SAT, acceptance rate, US News/THE/QS/CSRankings |
| **AAU Membership** | Embedded list (~71 institutions) | Stable; update when new members added |
| **Common App / Coalition / etc.** | Embedded lists | Update annually from organization websites |

### Approach for Non-Downloadable Sources

For sources that require scraping, the importer will:
1. **Try automated download first** (known API/URL patterns)
2. **Fall back to cached local file** if download fails
3. **Document manual steps** in `DATA_SOURCES.md` with exact commands
4. **Accept `--file` flag** so user can provide manually-obtained data

For scrapers specifically:
- Use `net/http` + `github.com/PuerkitoBio/goquery` for HTML table extraction
- Use `chromedp` only if JavaScript rendering is required
- Keep scraping code isolated in each importer package for easy updates

## Key Files to Reference

- `tools/samplesql/main.go` ‚Äî CLI pattern, flag parsing, DB connection
- `tools/samplesql/extractor.go` ‚Äî pgx query patterns, column detection
- `api/internal/db/db.go` ‚Äî Connection with retry pattern
- `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql` ‚Äî Complete target schema
- `api/internal/search/registry.go` ‚Äî JSONB paths the API queries (consumer contract)

## Issue Breakdown

### Epic: Data Import Tool (`tools/importer`)

---

#### 1. Scaffold importer module
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: none
- **Description**: Create `tools/importer/` Go module with `go.mod`
  (`github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer`), `main.go` with subcommand
  dispatch via stdlib `flag`, `internal/config/config.go` (DATABASE_URL,
  CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN flags + env fallback). Include `Importer`
  interface definition in `registry.go`. Compile and run `kapow-import --help`.
- **Done when**: `go build ./...` succeeds, `kapow-import --help` prints usage.

#### 2. Implement internal/dbutil
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: `internal/dbutil/` package: `connect.go` (pgxpool with retry,
  reuse pattern from `api/internal/db/db.go`), `upsert.go` (UpsertInstitution
  with JSONB merge via `COALESCE(col,'{}'::jsonb) || $N`, UpsertDataset,
  UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB), `batch.go` (CopyFrom wrapper, BatchExec
  using pgx.Batch), `views.go` (RefreshMaterializedViews for dataset_merged +
  dataset_latest). Include Institution/Dataset/AnnualDataset Go structs.
  Integration tests against dev DB.
- **Done when**: Integration test creates/updates an institution + dataset + refreshes views.

#### 3. Implement internal/fetch
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: `internal/fetch/` package: HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
  conditional requests, cache keyed by SHA-256 of URL under `~/.cache/kapow-import/`,
  transparent .zip/.gz/.xz decompression via `GetReader()`, `--file` local
  override bypass, stale-cache-on-failure resilience. Unit tests with
  `httptest.Server`.
- **Done when**: Unit tests pass for cache hit, miss, conditional 304, decompression, local fallback.

#### 4. Implement internal/csvutil
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: `internal/csvutil/` package: header-mapped CSV/TSV reader
  wrapping `encoding/csv`. `NewReader(r, opts...)` with `WithDelimiter`,
  `WithLazyQuotes`. `Row` type with `String(col)`, `Int(col)`, `Float(col)`,
  `OptionalString(col)`, `OptionalInt(col)`, `OptionalFloat(col)`. Handle
  missing columns gracefully (return zero/nil). Unit tests with embedded
  test CSV data.
- **Done when**: Unit tests cover normal parsing, TSV, missing columns, type coercion, quoted values.

#### 5. Implement internal/namematch (Tier 1 ‚Äî deterministic)
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 2
- **Description**: `internal/namematch/` package. `matcher.go`: Load all
  institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. 3-tier lookup: exact
  lowercased name ‚Üí normalized name ‚Üí alias. Also `MatchByOPE6`/`MatchByOPE8`.
  `normalize.go`: lowercase, strip diacritics (x/text/transform), remove "The ",
  strip punctuation, collapse whitespace. `aliases.go`: ~1000 hardcoded alias
  overrides for institutions whose external names differ from NCES canonical
  names (e.g. "Arizona State University" ‚Üí "Arizona State University - Tempe").
  Unit tests for normalization; integration test for full matcher load + lookup.
- **Done when**: Unit tests pass; integration test resolves known aliases ‚Üí correct unit_id.

#### 6. Implement NCES College Scorecard importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P0
- **Deps**: 2, 3, 4
- **Description**: `nces/` package. Downloads College Scorecard CSV from
  `collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/`. `fields.go`: maps 200+ CSV columns to JSONB
  keys (demographics, test scores, costs, admissions, programs). `nces.go`:
  parses CSV, filters (skip non-degree-granting, graduate-only, missing OPE IDs),
  handles OPE6/OPE8 deduplication (prefer lower unit_id), inserts states into
  `location.state`, creates institutions with coordinates + nces JSONB, creates
  CORE and ACADEMICS datasets. Unit tests with 10-row fixture CSV; integration
  test against dev DB.
- **Done when**: Integration test imports fixture ‚Üí institutions + datasets exist in DB with correct JSONB.

#### 7. Implement internal/ranking builder
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 2, 5
- **Description**: `internal/ranking/` package. `Opinion` struct (publication/
  category slugs, method rank/score/tier/range, year, unit_id, rank, score, tier,
  rank range). `Builder` accumulates opinions, `Flush(ctx)` batch-writes:
  upsert ranking metadata into `search_base.ranking`, upsert into
  `search_base.annual_dataset` (theme=RANKING), merge into
  `institution.rankings` JSONB. Implement `deriveOpinion()` to parse numeric
  ranks, tier strings ("Tier 1"), and ranges ("201-300"). Unit tests for opinion
  derivation; integration test for flush.
- **Done when**: Integration test flushes 10 opinions ‚Üí ranking + annual_dataset + institution.rankings populated.

#### 8. Implement NCES Field of Study importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 6
- **Description**: `ncesfos/` package. Parses FieldOfStudyData CSV. Maps
  credential level codes (1=CERT, 2=ASSOC, 3=BACH, 4=MAST, 5=DOCT, 6=PROF).
  Groups by unit_id + CIP code. Writes to `institution.fields_of_study` JSONB
  and inserts into `search_base.cip_data`. Uses canonicalUnitId() for OPE
  normalization. Unit test with fixture; integration test.
- **Done when**: cip_data table populated, institution.fields_of_study has grouped CIP data.

#### 9. Implement US News ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/usnews.go`. Parses historical CSV (1984‚Äì2024, from
  frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings or local file). Handles numeric
  ranks and tier strings. Multiple categories (National Universities, Liberal
  Arts). Uses name matcher. Feeds RankingBuilder. Unit test with fixture rows.
- **Done when**: Rankings for US News categories appear in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 10. Implement NCAA athletics importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 5, 6
- **Description**: `ncaa/` package. `sports.go`: maps 38 sports to gender
  (men/women/coed), football subdivision detection (FBS/FCS). `ncaa.go`: parses
  CSV, 3-tier name matching, inserts conferences into `search_base.conference`,
  writes `institution.athletics` JSONB + ATHLETICS dataset. Document NCAA
  Directory as data source (manual CSV export from web3.ncaa.org or scrape).
  Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: conference table populated, athletics JSONB has division/conference/sports.

#### 11. Implement Weather/Koppen importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 6
- **Description**: `weather/` package. Parses Koppen-Geiger ASCII text file
  (lat, lon, classification). `grid.go`: normalizes institution coordinates to
  0.25/0.75 grid, spiral search up to 3 degrees for nearest match. Writes
  `institution.weather` JSONB + WEATHER dataset. Unit test for grid/spiral logic;
  integration test.
- **Done when**: institution.weather has Koppen classification for all institutions with coordinates.

#### 12. Implement ZIP coordinates importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P1
- **Deps**: 2, 4
- **Description**: `zipcoords/` package. Parses CSV (ZIP, lat, lon, municipality,
  state). Bulk inserts into `location.zip_coordinates` with PostGIS geometry
  (`ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(lon,lat),4326)`). Uses CopyFrom for performance.
  Unit test for CSV parsing; integration test.
- **Done when**: zip_coordinates table populated with ~42,000 rows.

#### 13. Implement Forbes ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/forbes.go`. Downloads from Forbes API JSON endpoint.
  Parses organization name + rank. Uses name matcher ‚Üí RankingBuilder. Unit test.
- **Done when**: Forbes rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 14. Implement Niche ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7, 3
- **Description**: `rankings/niche.go`. Paginated API scrape (109 pages, needs
  User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests). Parses JSON
  entities with IPEDS IDs (direct match, no name matching needed). Feeds
  RankingBuilder. Document scraping procedure in DATA_SOURCES.md.
- **Done when**: Niche rankings imported; scraping documented.

#### 15. Implement Times Higher Ed ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/timeshighered.go`. Parses JSON. Handles numeric
  ranks and tier bands ("400-500"). Includes overall score. Filters "Reporter"
  entries. Name matching via aliases. Unit test.
- **Done when**: THE rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 16. Implement QS World ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7, 4
- **Description**: `rankings/qs.go`. Parses TSV. Handles tied ranks (trailing
  '='). Multiple years per file. Overall score included. Unit test.
- **Done when**: QS rankings imported for multiple years.

#### 17. Implement Washington Monthly ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/washmonthly.go`. Parses JSON. Category-based
  (national universities, liberal arts). Uses unit_id from JSON when available,
  name matching as fallback. Unit test.
- **Done when**: WashMonthly rankings in JSONB by category.

#### 18. Implement WSJ ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/wsj.go`. Parses JSON `{ colleges: [...] }` with
  unitid + rank. Direct unit_id matching + name fallback. Unit test.
- **Done when**: WSJ rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.

#### 19. Implement ARWU/Shanghai ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7, 3
- **Description**: `rankings/arwu.go`. Scrapes ranking table from
  `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` using goquery or downloads PDF.
  Parses name + national rank + total score. Name matching. Document scraping
  procedure. Also support local JSON file for pre-scraped data.
- **Done when**: ARWU rankings imported; scraping documented in DATA_SOURCES.md.

#### 20. Implement Money ranking importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 7
- **Description**: `rankings/money.go`. Embedded data for current year.
  Also document HTML scraping procedure for future updates (goquery on
  `money.com/best-colleges/`, extract `tr[data-id]`). Unit test.
- **Done when**: Money rankings imported from embedded data.

#### 21. Implement Crime/Clery Act importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 6, 4
- **Description**: `crime/` package. Parses multiple CSVs (on-campus crime,
  non-campus crime, on-campus VAWA, non-campus VAWA ‚Äî converted from XLS via
  libreoffice). Deduplicates by OPE8 preferring Main Campus. Aggregates crime
  categories (murder, rape, robbery, etc.) and VAWA categories (dating violence,
  stalking). Writes `institution.safety_and_security` JSONB + SAFETY dataset.
  Document XLS‚ÜíCSV conversion step. Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: safety_and_security JSONB populated with crime + VAWA counts.

#### 22. Implement Admissions importer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
- **Deps**: 5, 6
- **Description**: `admissions/` package. `platforms.go`: embedded membership
  lists for Common App (~1500), Coalition (~150), ApplyTexas, Apply SUNY, UC
  system, CSU system. `admissions.go`: determines platform membership by
  name/alias matching + prefix detection (UC/CSU). Writes
  `institution.admissions` JSONB + ADMISSIONS dataset. Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: admissions JSONB has application platform flags for matched institutions.

#### 23. Implement Misc importers (partisan lean, AAU, metro)
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 6
- **Description**: `misc/` package. Three sub-importers:
  `partisan.go` ‚Äî parses FiveThirtyEight CSV, PostGIS spatial join to
  congressional districts. `aau.go` ‚Äî embedded list of ~71 AAU members, name
  matching. `metro.go` ‚Äî CBSA/metro area lookup via PostGIS spatial query.
  All write to `institution.misc` JSONB + CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT dataset.
  Unit tests for parsing; integration test for PostGIS joins.
- **Done when**: misc JSONB has partisan_lean, aau_member, metro_area fields.

#### 24. Implement Name Normalizer
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 5, 6 (ideally all importers complete, but not a hard dep)
- **Description**: `normalizer/` package. Reads all institutions from DB, runs
  `namematch.Normalize(name)`, detects collisions, writes to
  `location.institution_normalized_alias` (unit_id, normalized_alias, city,
  state_code). Should run last in the pipeline. Unit + integration tests.
- **Done when**: institution_normalized_alias table populated for all institutions.

#### 25. Implement run-all pipeline
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: all importers (6‚Äì24)
- **Description**: `run-all` subcommand in `registry.go`. Executes importers in
  dependency order (Layer 0: nces + zipcoords ‚Üí Layer 1: all others ‚Üí Layer 2:
  normalizer ‚Üí Final: RefreshMaterializedViews). Supports `--skip=weather,metro`
  and `--only=nces,rankings` flags. Logs per-importer timing and summary stats.
  Stops on first error (with `--continue-on-error` flag to override).
- **Done when**: `kapow-import run-all` populates a fresh DB end-to-end.

#### 26. Implement namematch Tier 2 (fuzzy matching)
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 5
- **Description**: `internal/namematch/fuzzy.go`. Uses `adrg/strutil` for
  Jaro-Winkler similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all
  institutions (pre-filtered by state when available from source data). Returns
  top candidates above threshold (0.92) with confidence scores. Logs unmatched
  names + fuzzy candidates to a JSON report file for review.
- **Done when**: Unit test shows fuzzy match for misspelled institution name; report file generated.

#### 27. Implement match-review subcommand
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
- **Deps**: 26
- **Description**: `match-review` subcommand. Loads unmatched name report from
  last import run. Displays each unmatched name with top fuzzy candidates.
  `--approve ID` persists a match as a new alias in `search_base.institution_alias`.
  `--reject ID` marks as known non-match (skip in future runs). Tracks review
  state in a local JSON file.
- **Done when**: Can review unmatched names interactively and persist aliases.

#### 28. Implement namematch Tier 3 (LLM-assisted)
- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
- **Deps**: 27
- **Description**: `internal/namematch/llm.go`. `--llm-assist` flag on
  match-review. Batches unmatched names with context (source, state, ranking,
  fuzzy candidates) and sends to Claude API. Presents LLM suggestions alongside
  fuzzy candidates in match-review. Uses `anthropic-sdk-go`. All matches still
  require human approval.
- **Done when**: LLM suggestions appear in match-review for unresolved names.

#### 29. Write DATA_SOURCES.md
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 6 (enough context to write; update as importers are added)
- **Description**: `tools/importer/DATA_SOURCES.md`. Complete documentation of
  every data source: URL, access method (download/API/scrape), file format,
  update cadence with expected + by dates, manual steps where automation isn't
  possible (XLS‚ÜíCSV conversion, NCAA directory export, ARWU scraping commands).
  Include the annual import schedule.
- **Done when**: Every data source documented with actionable instructions.

#### 30. Update Makefile and documentation
- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
- **Deps**: 1
- **Description**: Add to root Makefile: `importer-build` (compiles binary),
  `importer-test` (runs tests), `importer-test-unit` (short tests). Update
  `tools/CLAUDE.md` with importer documentation. Update root `CLAUDE.md` if
  needed (add importer to "Common task recipes").
- **Done when**: `make importer-build` and `make importer-test-unit` work from root.

---

### Dependency Graph

```

## Verification

content.text:
Two good questions:

### 1. Source data acquisition

The design doc (`docs/plans/kapow-o0j-data-import-tool.md:206-249`) does cover this ‚Äî it categorizes sources into three tiers:

- **Programmatic download** (lines 208-218): College Scorecard, Forbes API, Niche API, Crime data, FiveThirtyEight, ZIP coords, K√∂ppen ‚Äî with actual URLs and formats
- **Web scraping required** (lines 222-231): ARWU, NCAA, Money, Princeton Review, THE, QS, WashMonthly, WSJ ‚Äî with method notes
- **Pre-curated** (lines 235-239): US News historical (GitHub CSV), AAU list, admissions platform lists

There's also a planned `DATA_SOURCES.md` (issue #29 in the doc) that would be the comprehensive, actionable reference with exact commands, but it **doesn't exist yet**. The design doc has enough info to get started, but it's scattered across the plan rather than being a standalone operator's guide.

### 2. Why no ready issues

`bd list` returns **zero issues** ‚Äî there are no beads issues at all, not just none in "ready" status. The 30-task breakdown in the design doc was never created as beads issues (or they were cleaned up at some point). The issue IDs referenced in the earlier exploration (kapow-89va, kapow-5adf, etc.) don't exist in the system.

**To unblock the importer work**, you'd need to either:
- Create beads issues from the design doc's 30-task breakdown
- Or just start building directly from the plan

Want me to create the beads issues from the design doc, or would you prefer to tackle something else first?

/home/ketan/project/kapow/PRODUCT.md:Finding the right college is one of the highest-stakes decisions a family makes, yet the information needed to make it well is scattered across dozens of websites, paywalled rankings, and opaque government data portals. A parent trying to answer "what small colleges in the Northeast have strong engineering programs, a graduation rate above 80%, and a net price under $30,000?" has to visit IPEDS, College Scorecard, US News, Niche, each school's website, and still piece it together manually.

content.text:
Perfect! I have enough information. Let me compile my findings.

## Summary: Institution Logos, Icons, Images, and Branding in Kapow

**The codebase has NO existing support for institution logos.** Here's what I found:

### 1. **Database Schema (PostgreSQL)**
- The `search_base.institution` table has **NO logo, icon, image, or badge columns**
- Only columns present: `unit_id, ope6_id, ope8_id, created, updated, version, institution_name, city, state, coordinates, nces, weather, rankings, athletics, admissions, fields_of_study, misc, safety_and_security`
- The `misc` JSONB column is a generic catch-all but no logo-related data is currently stored there

**Migration file**: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql` (lines 42‚Äì62)

### 2. **GraphQL API Schema**
- **No institution logo fields** in the GraphQL schema
- The `InstitutionDetail` type only includes: `unitID, name, city, state, latitude, longitude, institutionType, enrollment, graduationRate, admissionRate, inStateTuition, outOfStateTuition, avgNetPrice, retentionRate, studentFacultyRatio`
- Only **user avatars** exist for authentication: `avatarUrl: String` on the `User` type (sourced from identity provider like Supabase)

**GraphQL files**: 
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/graph/schema/institution.graphql` (minimal)
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/schema.graphql` (compiled schema)

### 3. **Frontend Components**
- No institution logo display components exist
- User avatars are handled in `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/components/auth/UserMenu.tsx` via `Avatar` from Mantine
- Institution cards and details (`InstitutionCard.tsx`, `InstitutionDetail.tsx`) don't reference logos

**Relevant files**:
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/components/search/InstitutionDetail.tsx` (modal detail view)
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/components/search/InstitutionCard.tsx` (search result card)

### 4. **Data Sources**
- The `ranking` table has a `url` column (line 102 of migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql), but this is for ranking publication URLs, not institution logos
- No references to external logo APIs (Clearbit, Wikipedia, College Scorecard) in the codebase
- External data sources documented are NCES College Scorecard, Clery Act data, and various ranking services‚Äînone provide logos

### 5. **Tests & Fixtures**
- Fixture data in `/home/ketan/project/kapow/common/src/main/resources/data/sample-100.sql` has no logo columns

### 6. **Documentation**
- No plan or spec documents mention institution logos
- Product overview mentions "source badges and tooltips" but only for data source attribution, not institution branding

---

**Conclusion**: Logos are completely absent from Kapow. To add them, you would need to:
1. Add a migration to add `logo_url` or similar column to `search_base.institution`
2. Update the GraphQL schema
3. Create frontend components to display logos
4. Decide on a logo source (external API, user-provided, hardcoded URLs, etc.)

## Summary: Institution Logos, Icons, Images, and Branding in Kapow

**The codebase has NO existing support for institution logos.** Here's what I found:

### 1. **Database Schema (PostgreSQL)**

### 2. **GraphQL API Schema**

**GraphQL files**:

### 3. **Frontend Components**
- No institution logo display components exist

- Institution cards and details (`InstitutionCard.tsx`, `InstitutionDetail.tsx`) don't reference logos

**Relevant files**:

### 4. **Data Sources**
- The `ranking` table has a `url` column (line 102 of migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql), but this is for ranking publication URLs, not institution logos
- No references to external logo APIs (Clearbit, Wikipedia, College Scorecard) in the codebase

### 5. **Tests & Fixtures**
- Fixture data in `/home/ketan/project/kapow/common/src/main/resources/data/sample-100.sql` has no logo columns

### 6. **Documentation**

---

**Conclusion**: Logos are completely absent from Kapow. To add them, you would need to:
1. Add a migration to add `logo_url` or similar column to `search_base.institution`
2. Update the GraphQL schema
3. Create frontend components to display logos
4. Decide on a logo source (external API, user-provided, hardcoded URLs, etc.)

No ‚Äî there's nothing in the codebase for institution logos. No database column, no GraphQL field, no frontend display, no data source for them.

For sourcing logos, a few options worth considering:

| Source | Coverage | Quality | Cost | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Clearbit Logo API** | High (by domain) | Good | Free tier available | Needs institution website domain |
| **Wikipedia/Wikidata** | ~80% of US colleges | Variable | Free | Requires SPARQL or page scraping |
| **College Scorecard** | N/A | N/A | N/A | Doesn't provide logos |
| **Institution websites** | 100% (manual) | Best | Free but labor-intensive | Scrape `/favicon.ico` or find seal/logo |
| **NCES IPEDS** | N/A | N/A | N/A | No logo data |

The most practical approach would probably be Clearbit (by domain name) or favicon scraping ‚Äî College Scorecard data includes institution website URLs, which gives you a domain to work with.

Want me to explore this further or is this a lower priority than getting the importers stood up?

content.text: The NCES JSONB has a `school_url` field (e.g., `"www.cacc.edu/"`). So we have website URLs for all institutions.

input.command: docker exec priceless_sanderson psql -U postgres -d kapow -c "SELECT count(*), count(nces->>'school_url') AS has_url FROM search_base.institution;" 2>&1

content.text:
6,038 out of 6,040 institutions have a `school_url` in their NCES JSONB. That's our input for favicon scraping.

**Summary of what we have to work with:**
- `nces->>'school_url'` ‚Äî e.g., `"www.cacc.edu/"` (no protocol prefix in most cases)
- 6,038 URLs available
- 2 institutions missing URLs

Want me to plan and build the favicon scraper script? I'm thinking a Go tool in `tools/` that:
1. Queries all institution `school_url` values from the DB
2. Fetches favicon for each (try `/favicon.ico`, then parse `<link rel="icon">` from HTML)
3. Saves to a local directory keyed by `unit_id`
4. Normalizes to PNG at a standard size

1‚Üípackage main
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"context"
     5‚Üí	"flag"
     6‚Üí	"fmt"
     7‚Üí	"log/slog"
     8‚Üí	"os"
     9‚Üí	"os/signal"
    10‚Üí	"strings"
    11‚Üí	"syscall"
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí	"github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer/internal/config"
    14‚Üí	"github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer/internal/dbutil"
    15‚Üí	"github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer/internal/fetch"
    16‚Üí)
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üífunc main() {
    19‚Üí	if len(os.Args) < 2 {
    20‚Üí		printUsage()
    21‚Üí		os.Exit(1)
    22‚Üí	}
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí	cmd := os.Args[1]
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí	// Handle help and sources before flag parsing.
    27‚Üí	switch cmd {
    28‚Üí	case "-h", "--help", "help":
    29‚Üí		printUsage()
    30‚Üí		return
    31‚Üí	case "sources":
    32‚Üí		printSources()
    33‚Üí		return
    34‚Üí	}
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí	cfg := &config.Config{}
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí	// Check for a registered importer first.
    39‚Üí	if imp := Get(cmd); imp != nil {
    40‚Üí		fs := flag.NewFlagSet(cmd, flag.ExitOnError)
    41‚Üí		cfg.RegisterFlags(fs)
    42‚Üí		imp.RegisterFlags(fs)
    43‚Üí		if err := fs.Parse(os.Args[2:]); err != nil {
    44‚Üí			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: %v\n", err)
    45‚Üí			os.Exit(1)
    46‚Üí		}
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí		setupLogging(cfg)
    49‚Üí		if err := runImporter(imp, cfg); err != nil {
    50‚Üí			slog.Error("import failed", "importer", cmd, "error", err)
    51‚Üí			os.Exit(1)
    52‚Üí		}
    53‚Üí		return
    54‚Üí	}
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí	// Built-in commands.
    57‚Üí	switch cmd {
    58‚Üí	case "run-all":
    59‚Üí		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("run-all", flag.ExitOnError)
    60‚Üí		cfg.RegisterFlags(fs)
    61‚Üí		skipFlag := fs.String("skip", "", "comma-separated importers to skip")
    62‚Üí		onlyFlag := fs.String("only", "", "comma-separated importers to run (exclusive)")
    63‚Üí		if err := fs.Parse(os.Args[2:]); err != nil {
    64‚Üí			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: %v\n", err)
    65‚Üí			os.Exit(1)
    66‚Üí		}
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí		setupLogging(cfg)
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí		skip := parseCSV(*skipFlag)
    71‚Üí		only := parseCSV(*onlyFlag)
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí		if err := runAll(cfg, skip, only); err != nil {
    74‚Üí			slog.Error("run-all failed", "error", err)
    75‚Üí			os.Exit(1)
    76‚Üí		}
    77‚Üí	default:
    78‚Üí		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "unknown command: %s\n\n", cmd)
    79‚Üí		printUsage()
    80‚Üí		os.Exit(1)
    81‚Üí	}
    82‚Üí}
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üífunc runImporter(imp Importer, cfg *config.Config) error {
    85‚Üí	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
    86‚Üí	defer cancel()
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí	if cfg.DryRun {
    89‚Üí		slog.Info("dry-run mode enabled ‚Äî no database writes")
    90‚Üí		fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
    91‚Üí		return imp.Run(ctx, cfg, nil, fetcher)
    92‚Üí	}
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí	if cfg.DatabaseURL == "" {
    95‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("importer: DATABASE_URL is required (set via --database-url or env)")
    96‚Üí	}
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí	pool, err := dbutil.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
    99‚Üí	if err != nil {
   100‚Üí		return err
   101‚Üí	}
   102‚Üí	defer pool.Close()
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí	fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
   105‚Üí	return imp.Run(ctx, cfg, pool, fetcher)
   106‚Üí}
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üífunc runAll(cfg *config.Config, skip, only map[string]bool) error {
   109‚Üí	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   110‚Üí	defer cancel()
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí	if cfg.DatabaseURL == "" {
   113‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("importer: DATABASE_URL is required")
   114‚Üí	}
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí	pool, err := dbutil.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
   117‚Üí	if err != nil {
   118‚Üí		return err
   119‚Üí	}
   120‚Üí	defer pool.Close()
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üí	fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí	if err := RunAll(ctx, cfg, pool, fetcher, skip, only); err != nil {
   125‚Üí		return err
   126‚Üí	}
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üí	slog.Info("refreshing materialized views")
   129‚Üí	return dbutil.RefreshMaterializedViews(ctx, pool)
   130‚Üí}
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üífunc setupLogging(cfg *config.Config) {
   133‚Üí	level := cfg.SlogLevel()
   134‚Üí	handler := slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: level})
   135‚Üí	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(handler))
   136‚Üí}
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üífunc parseCSV(s string) map[string]bool {
   139‚Üí	m := make(map[string]bool)
   140‚Üí	if s == "" {
   141‚Üí		return m
   142‚Üí	}
   143‚Üí	for _, v := range strings.Split(s, ",") {
   144‚Üí		v = strings.TrimSpace(v)
   145‚Üí		if v != "" {
   146‚Üí			m[v] = true
   147‚Üí		}
   148‚Üí	}
   149‚Üí	return m
   150‚Üí}
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üífunc printUsage() {
   153‚Üí	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, `kapow-import ‚Äî Kapow data import tool
   154‚Üí
   155‚ÜíUsage: kapow-import <command> [flags]
   156‚Üí
   157‚ÜíCommands:
   158‚Üí`)
   159‚Üí	for _, imp := range registry {
   160‚Üí		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  %-16s %s\n", imp.Name(), imp.Description())
   161‚Üí	}
   162‚Üí	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, `  %-16s Run all importers in dependency order
   163‚Üí  %-16s Print data source documentation
   164‚Üí
   165‚ÜíGlobal flags:
   166‚Üí  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
   167‚Üí  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow-import)
   168‚Üí  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
   169‚Üí  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB
   170‚Üí`, "run-all", "sources")
   171‚Üí}
   172‚Üí
   173‚Üífunc printSources() {
   174‚Üí	fmt.Println("See DATA_SOURCES.md in this directory for complete data source documentation.")
   175‚Üí}

1‚Üípackage config
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (

     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí// Config holds global configuration for the importer.
    10‚Üítype Config struct {
    11‚Üí	DatabaseURL string
    12‚Üí	CacheDir    string
    13‚Üí	LogLevel    string
    14‚Üí	DryRun      bool
    15‚Üí}
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí// RegisterFlags adds global flags to the given FlagSet.
    18‚Üífunc (c *Config) RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet) {
    19‚Üí	fs.StringVar(&c.DatabaseURL, "database-url", os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL"), "PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)")
    20‚Üí	fs.StringVar(&c.CacheDir, "cache-dir", defaultCacheDir(), "download cache directory")
    21‚Üí	fs.StringVar(&c.LogLevel, "log-level", "info", "log level: debug|info|warn|error")
    22‚Üí	fs.BoolVar(&c.DryRun, "dry-run", false, "parse and validate without writing to DB")
    23‚Üí}
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí// SlogLevel returns the slog.Level for the configured log level string.
    26‚Üífunc (c *Config) SlogLevel() slog.Level {
    27‚Üí	switch c.LogLevel {
    28‚Üí	case "debug":
    29‚Üí		return slog.LevelDebug
    30‚Üí	case "warn":
    31‚Üí		return slog.LevelWarn
    32‚Üí	case "error":
    33‚Üí		return slog.LevelError
    34‚Üí	default:
    35‚Üí		return slog.LevelInfo
    36‚Üí	}
    37‚Üí}
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üífunc defaultCacheDir() string {

1‚Üípackage config
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"fmt"
     5‚Üí	"log/slog"
     6‚Üí	"strings"
     7‚Üí	"time"
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí	"github.com/caarlos0/env/v11"
    10‚Üí)
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí// Config holds all application configuration loaded from environment variables.
    13‚Üítype Config struct {
    14‚Üí	Host string `env:"HOST" envDefault:"0.0.0.0"`
    15‚Üí	Port int    `env:"PORT" envDefault:"8081"`
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí	DatabaseURL string `env:"DATABASE_URL,required"`
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí	JWTSecret    string `env:"JWT_SECRET"` //nolint:gosec // G117: config field name, not a hardcoded secret
    20‚Üí	JWTPublicKey string `env:"JWT_PUBLIC_KEY"`
    21‚Üí	JWTIssuer    string `env:"JWT_ISSUER"`
    22‚Üí	JWTAudience  string `env:"JWT_AUDIENCE"`
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí	OIDCDiscoveryURL string `env:"OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL"`
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí	LogLevel  string `env:"LOG_LEVEL" envDefault:"info"`
    27‚Üí	LogFormat string `env:"LOG_FORMAT" envDefault:"json"`
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí	Env string `env:"ENV" envDefault:"development"`
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí	CORSOrigins string `env:"CORS_ORIGINS"`
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí	GraphQLPlayground    bool `env:"GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND" envDefault:"false"`
    34‚Üí	GraphQLIntrospection bool `env:"GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION" envDefault:"true"`
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí	MCPEnabled  bool `env:"MCP_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    37‚Üí	OTelEnabled bool `env:"OTEL_ENABLED" envDefault:"false"`
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí	ShutdownTimeout time.Duration `env:"SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT" envDefault:"15s"`
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí	MaxExportRows int `env:"MAX_EXPORT_ROWS" envDefault:"500"`
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí	// Rate limiting
    44‚Üí	RateLimitEnabled     bool   `env:"RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    45‚Üí	RateLimitGraphQL     int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL" envDefault:"60"`
    46‚Üí	RateLimitGraphQLAuth int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH" envDefault:"120"`
    47‚Üí	RateLimitExport      int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT" envDefault:"10"`
    48‚Üí	RateLimitMCP         int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_MCP" envDefault:"120"`
    49‚Üí	PrivateAllowlistCIDRs string `env:"PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS" envDefault:"127.0.0.0/8,::1/128"`
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí	// HTTP debug logging
    52‚Üí	HTTPDebugRequestHeaders  string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS" envDefault:"none"`
    53‚Üí	HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_SELECTED_HEADERS" envDefault:"Content-Type,Accept,User-Agent"`
    54‚Üí	HTTPDebugRedactSensitive bool   `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_REDACT_SENSITIVE" envDefault:"true"`
    55‚Üí	HTTPDebugResponseMode    string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_RESPONSE_MODE" envDefault:"none"`
    56‚Üí	HTTPDebugBodyMaxBytes    int    `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_BODY_MAX_BYTES" envDefault:"4096"`
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí	// logLevelVar holds the dynamic log level. It is initialized by LogHandler
    59‚Üí	// and can be updated at runtime to change the global log level without a restart.
    60‚Üí	logLevelVar slog.LevelVar
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí	// swappableWriter is the log output target, swappable at runtime.
    63‚Üí	swappableWriter *SwappableWriter
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí	// categoryHandler wraps the slog handler with per-category level overrides.
    66‚Üí	categoryHandler *CategoryHandler
    67‚Üí}
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí// Load parses and validates configuration from environment variables.
    70‚Üífunc Load() (*Config, error) {
    71‚Üí	cfg := &Config{}
    72‚Üí	if err := env.Parse(cfg); err != nil {
    73‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("config: %w", err)
    74‚Üí	}
    75‚Üí	return cfg, nil
    76‚Üí}
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí// Addr returns the host:port bind address.
    79‚Üífunc (c *Config) Addr() string {
    80‚Üí	return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", c.Host, c.Port)
    81‚Üí}
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí// LevelTrace is a custom log level below DEBUG for very detailed diagnostic
    84‚Üí// output. Usage: logger.Log(ctx, config.LevelTrace, "msg", "key", val)
    85‚Üíconst LevelTrace = slog.Level(-8)
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí// ParseLogLevel converts a log level string to the corresponding slog.Level.
    88‚Üífunc ParseLogLevel(s string) slog.Level {
    89‚Üí	switch s {
    90‚Üí	case "trace":
    91‚Üí		return LevelTrace
    92‚Üí	case "debug":
    93‚Üí		return slog.LevelDebug
    94‚Üí	case "warn":
    95‚Üí		return slog.LevelWarn
    96‚Üí	case "error":
    97‚Üí		return slog.LevelError
    98‚Üí	default:
    99‚Üí		return slog.LevelInfo
   100‚Üí	}
   101‚Üí}
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí// LogHandler returns an slog.Handler configured per LOG_LEVEL and LOG_FORMAT.
   104‚Üí// The handler uses a dynamic LevelVar so the log level can be changed at
   105‚Üí// runtime via LogLevelVar().Set() without recreating the handler. Output is
   106‚Üí// routed through a SwappableWriter and wrapped with a CategoryHandler for
   107‚Üí// per-category level overrides.
   108‚Üífunc (c *Config) LogHandler() slog.Handler {
   109‚Üí	c.logLevelVar.Set(ParseLogLevel(c.LogLevel))
   110‚Üí	c.swappableWriter = NewSwappableWriter()
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí	opts := &slog.HandlerOptions{
   113‚Üí		Level:     &c.logLevelVar,
   114‚Üí		AddSource: c.Env == "development",
   115‚Üí		ReplaceAttr: func(groups []string, a slog.Attr) slog.Attr {
   116‚Üí			if a.Key == slog.LevelKey {
   117‚Üí				if level, ok := a.Value.Any().(slog.Level); ok && level == LevelTrace {
   118‚Üí					a.Value = slog.StringValue("TRACE")
   119‚Üí				}
   120‚Üí			}
   121‚Üí			return a
   122‚Üí		},
   123‚Üí	}
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí	var inner slog.Handler
   126‚Üí	if c.LogFormat == "text" {
   127‚Üí		inner = slog.NewTextHandler(c.swappableWriter, opts)
   128‚Üí	} else {
   129‚Üí		inner = slog.NewJSONHandler(c.swappableWriter, opts)
   130‚Üí	}
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí	c.categoryHandler = NewCategoryHandler(inner, &c.logLevelVar)
   133‚Üí	return c.categoryHandler
   134‚Üí}
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí// LogLevelVar returns the dynamic log level variable. Callers can change
   137‚Üí// the active log level at runtime by calling Set() on the returned LevelVar.
   138‚Üífunc (c *Config) LogLevelVar() *slog.LevelVar {
   139‚Üí	return &c.logLevelVar
   140‚Üí}
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí// LogWriter returns the swappable writer used for log output.
   143‚Üí// Returns nil if LogHandler has not been called yet.
   144‚Üífunc (c *Config) LogWriter() *SwappableWriter {
   145‚Üí	return c.swappableWriter
   146‚Üí}
   147‚Üí
   148‚Üí// LogCategoryHandler returns the category handler for per-category level overrides.
   149‚Üí// Returns nil if LogHandler has not been called yet.
   150‚Üífunc (c *Config) LogCategoryHandler() *CategoryHandler {
   151‚Üí	return c.categoryHandler
   152‚Üí}
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí// HTTPDebugEnabled reports whether any HTTP debug logging is configured.
   155‚Üífunc (c *Config) HTTPDebugEnabled() bool {
   156‚Üí	return c.HTTPDebugRequestHeaders != "none" || c.HTTPDebugResponseMode != "none"
   157‚Üí}
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí// ParsedSelectedHeaders splits the comma-separated HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders
   160‚Üí// into a trimmed slice of header names.
   161‚Üífunc (c *Config) ParsedSelectedHeaders() []string {
   162‚Üí	raw := strings.Split(c.HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders, ",")
   163‚Üí	out := make([]string, 0, len(raw))
   164‚Üí	for _, h := range raw {
   165‚Üí		h = strings.TrimSpace(h)
   166‚Üí		if h != "" {
   167‚Üí			out = append(out, h)
   168‚Üí		}
   169‚Üí	}
   170‚Üí	return out
   171‚Üí}
   172‚Üí

1‚Üípackage server
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"context"
     5‚Üí	"fmt"
     6‚Üí	"log/slog"
     7‚Üí	"net/http"
     8‚Üí	"time"
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/config"
    11‚Üí)
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üíconst (
    14‚Üí	readTimeout       = 10 * time.Second
    15‚Üí	writeTimeout      = 30 * time.Second
    16‚Üí	idleTimeout       = 120 * time.Second
    17‚Üí	readHeaderTimeout = 5 * time.Second
    18‚Üí)
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí// New constructs an *http.Server with production-grade timeouts.
    21‚Üífunc New(cfg *config.Config, handler http.Handler) *http.Server {
    22‚Üí	return &http.Server{
    23‚Üí		Addr:              cfg.Addr(),
    24‚Üí		Handler:           handler,
    25‚Üí		ReadTimeout:       readTimeout,
    26‚Üí		WriteTimeout:      writeTimeout,
    27‚Üí		IdleTimeout:       idleTimeout,
    28‚Üí		ReadHeaderTimeout: readHeaderTimeout,
    29‚Üí	}
    30‚Üí}
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí// Shutdown gracefully drains in-flight requests within the configured timeout.
    33‚Üífunc Shutdown(srv *http.Server, timeout time.Duration) error {
    34‚Üí	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)
    35‚Üí	defer cancel()
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí	slog.Info("server: shutting down", "timeout", timeout)
    38‚Üí	if err := srv.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {
    39‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("server: shutdown error: %w", err)
    40‚Üí	}
    41‚Üí	slog.Info("server: shutdown complete")
    42‚Üí	return nil
    43‚Üí}
    44‚Üí

1‚Üí# Kapow ‚Äî College Power Search
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíA college/university search platform with a Go API backend and React frontend.
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí## Quick Start with Docker (recommended)
     6‚Üí
     7‚ÜíThe fastest way to try Kapow ‚Äî a single Docker image bundles PostgreSQL, the API
     8‚Üíserver, and the web frontend with pre-loaded fixture data.
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí```bash
    11‚Üí# Build the all-in-one image
    12‚Üímake test-image-build
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üí# Run it (exposes port 8080)
    15‚Üímake test-image-run
    16‚Üí```
    17‚Üí
    18‚ÜíOpen **http://localhost:8080** in your browser. To run the interactive demo
    19‚Üíagainst it, see [DEMO.md](DEMO.md).
    20‚Üí
    21‚ÜíThe container includes:
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí- PostgreSQL 15 + PostGIS with pre-loaded college data
    24‚Üí- Go API server on internal port 8081
    25‚Üí- nginx reverse proxy serving the React frontend and proxying API requests
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíNo configuration needed ‚Äî the container handles everything on first boot,
    28‚Üíincluding database initialization and fixture data loading.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíTo stop: press `Ctrl+C`.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí---
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí## Devcontainer
    35‚Üí
    36‚ÜíSee [.devcontainer/README.md](.devcontainer/README.md) for setup instructions
    37‚Üí(GitHub Codespaces, VS Code, and CLI).
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí---
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí## Local Development Setup
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí### Prerequisites
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí- **Go** 1.23+
    46‚Üí- **Node.js** 22+ with **pnpm** 9+ (`npm install -g pnpm`)
    47‚Üí- **Docker** (for the dev database container)
    48‚Üí- **psql** (PostgreSQL client) ‚Äî `brew install libpq` on macOS, `apt install postgresql-client` on Linux
    49‚Üí
    50‚ÜíOptional:
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí- **golangci-lint** for linting
    53‚Üí- **air** for standalone API hot-reload: `go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest` (`make dev` uses `go run` instead, no install needed)
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí### Quick start (recommended)
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíA single command starts everything ‚Äî PostgreSQL in Docker, the Go API with hot
    58‚Üíreload, and the Vite dev server:
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí```bash
    61‚Üímake dev
    62‚Üí```
    63‚Üí
    64‚ÜíThis will:
    65‚Üí- Start a PostgreSQL + PostGIS container on port **15432** (won't conflict with a host PostgreSQL)
    66‚Üí- Load fixture data on first run (subsequent runs skip this step)
    67‚Üí- Run database migrations
    68‚Üí- Start the API with **air** (hot reload) on **:8081**
    69‚Üí- Start the web dev server on **:8080** with HMR
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíOpen **http://localhost:8080** and you're ready to go. Press **Ctrl+C** to stop
    72‚Üíeverything. The database volume persists between runs, so the next `make dev`
    73‚Üístarts much faster.
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí```bash
    76‚Üímake dev-clean    # remove this checkout's DB volume (forces fresh fixture load)
    77‚Üímake dev-prune    # remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
    78‚Üí```
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí### Manual setup (alternative)
    81‚Üí
    82‚ÜíIf you prefer to manage PostgreSQL yourself:
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí#### 1. Configure environment
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí```bash
    87‚Üícp .env.example .env
    88‚Üí```
    89‚Üí
    90‚ÜíEdit `.env` and set at minimum:
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí```
    93‚ÜíDATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/kapow?sslmode=disable
    94‚ÜíJWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret
    95‚Üí```
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí#### 2. Set up the database
    98‚Üí
    99‚ÜíCreate the database, then run migrations:
   100‚Üí

1‚Üí# Kapow Data Import Tool ‚Äî Design Document
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üí## Context
     4‚Üí
     5‚ÜíKapow (College Power Search) has a fully defined PostgreSQL schema and a working
     6‚ÜíAPI/frontend, but no data import pipeline. The database needs to be populated
     7‚Üífrom government data (NCES College Scorecard, Clery Act crime stats), college
     8‚Üíranking publications (US News, Forbes, Niche, etc.), athletic conference data
     9‚Üí(NCAA), climate data (Koppen), and other sources. This tool provides a single Go
    10‚Üíbinary with subcommands to import all data sources, with download+cache support
    11‚Üíand proper documentation for sources that require web scraping.
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí## Architecture
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí### Module: `tools/importer/` (standalone Go module, like `tools/samplesql/`)
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí```
    18‚Üítools/importer/
    19‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ go.mod                       # github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer
    20‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ main.go                      # CLI entrypoint: subcommand dispatch
    21‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ registry.go                  # Importer registration table + run-all orchestrator
    22‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ DATA_SOURCES.md              # Data source URLs, scraping instructions, update cadence
    23‚Üí‚îÇ
    24‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ internal/
    25‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ config/config.go         # DATABASE_URL, CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN
    26‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ dbutil/
    27‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ connect.go           # pgxpool connection with retry
    28‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ upsert.go            # UpsertInstitution, UpsertDataset, UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB
    29‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ batch.go             # CopyFrom bulk insert, BatchExec
    30‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ views.go             # RefreshMaterializedViews
    31‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fetch/
    32‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fetch.go             # HTTP download + ETag cache + decompression + local fallback
    33‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ fetch_test.go
    34‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ csvutil/
    35‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ reader.go            # Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader with typed column access
    36‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ reader_test.go
    37‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ namematch/
    38‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ matcher.go           # Multi-tier institution name matching
    39‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ normalize.go         # Name canonicalization rules
    40‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ aliases.go           # Hardcoded alias overrides (~1000 institution name mappings)
    41‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fuzzy.go             # Tier 2: Jaro-Winkler fuzzy matching (adrg/strutil)
    42‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ llm.go               # Tier 3: LLM-assisted curation (Claude API, optional)
    43‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ matcher_test.go
    44‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ranking/
    45‚Üí‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ builder.go           # Opinion type, RankingBuilder: accumulate ‚Üí batch flush
    46‚Üí‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ builder_test.go
    47‚Üí‚îÇ
    48‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nces/                        # NCES College Scorecard (institution foundation)
    49‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nces.go
    50‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fields.go                # CSV column ‚Üí JSONB key mapping (200+ fields)
    51‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ nces_test.go
    52‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ncesfos/                     # NCES Field of Study (CIP codes)
    53‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ rankings/                    # All 9+ ranking publications (one file each)
    54‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ usnews.go, forbes.go, niche.go, timeshighered.go,
    55‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   qs.go, washmonthly.go, wsj.go, arwu.go, money.go
    56‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ rankings_test.go
    57‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ncaa/                        # NCAA athletics
    58‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ncaa.go
    59‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sports.go                # Sport‚Üígender/category mapping
    60‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ weather/                     # Koppen climate zones
    61‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ crime/                       # Clery Act crime stats
    62‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ admissions/                  # Application platforms (Common App, Coalition, etc.)
    63‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ platforms.go             # Embedded platform membership lists
    64‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ misc/                        # Partisan lean, AAU, metro area
    65‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ zipcoords/                   # ZIP code coordinates
    66‚Üí‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ normalizer/                  # institution_normalized_alias generator
    67‚Üí```
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí### CLI Design
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíSingle binary, subcommand dispatch via stdlib `flag`:
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí```
    74‚Üíkapow-import <command> [flags]
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíCommands:
    77‚Üí  nces             Import NCES College Scorecard institutions
    78‚Üí  nces-fos         Import NCES Field of Study data
    79‚Üí  rankings         Import rankings (--publication=usnews|forbes|niche|...)
    80‚Üí  ncaa             Import NCAA athletics data
    81‚Üí  weather          Import Koppen climate classification
    82‚Üí  crime            Import Clery Act crime statistics
    83‚Üí  admissions       Import application platform membership
    84‚Üí  misc             Import misc data (partisan lean, AAU, metro area)
    85‚Üí  zipcoords        Import ZIP code coordinates
    86‚Üí  normalizer       Generate normalized institution aliases
    87‚Üí  run-all          Run all importers in dependency order
    88‚Üí  match-review     Review unmatched names from last run (fuzzy + LLM suggestions)
    89‚Üí  sources          Print data source documentation
    90‚Üí
    91‚ÜíGlobal flags:
    92‚Üí  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
    93‚Üí  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow-import)
    94‚Üí  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
    95‚Üí  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB
    96‚Üí
    97‚ÜíPer-command flags:
    98‚Üí  --file PATH      Use local file instead of downloading
    99‚Üí  --year YYYY      Data year (where applicable)
   100‚Üí  rankings --publication SLUG [--category SLUG]
   101‚Üí  run-all --skip=weather,metro  |  --only=nces,rankings
   102‚Üí```
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí### Importer Interface
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí```go
   107‚Üítype Importer interface {
   108‚Üí    Name() string
   109‚Üí    Description() string
   110‚Üí    Dependencies() []string
   111‚Üí    RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet)
   112‚Üí    Run(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config, pool *pgxpool.Pool, fetcher *fetch.Fetcher) error
   113‚Üí}
   114‚Üí```
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí### Shared Infrastructure
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí**`internal/fetch`** ‚Äî Download + cache. HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
   119‚Üíconditional requests. Cache keyed by URL hash. Transparent .zip/.gz/.xz
   120‚Üídecompression. `--file` flag bypasses download. Stale cache preferred over
   121‚Üífailure when source is temporarily unavailable.
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí**`internal/csvutil`** ‚Äî Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader. `Row.String("COL")`,
   124‚Üí`Row.Int("COL")`, `Row.OptionalFloat("COL")` typed accessors. Avoids
   125‚Üírepetitive index-based column access.
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí**`internal/namematch`** ‚Äî Multi-tier institution name resolution:
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí- **Tier 1 ‚Äî Deterministic** (free, instant, ~95% of matches):
   130‚Üí  Loads all institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. Exact name ‚Üí
   131‚Üí  normalized name ‚Üí alias lookup. Also supports OPE6/OPE8 ID lookups.
   132‚Üí  Normalization: lowercase, strip diacritics, remove "The ", strip punctuation,
   133‚Üí  collapse whitespace. Includes ~1000 hardcoded alias overrides for institutions
   134‚Üí  whose external names differ from their NCES canonical names.
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí- **Tier 2 ‚Äî Fuzzy string similarity** (free, fast, ~3-4% more):
   137‚Üí  Uses [adrg/strutil](https://github.com/adrg/strutil) for Jaro-Winkler
   138‚Üí  similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all institutions
   139‚Üí  (pre-filtered by state when available). Matches above threshold (0.92) are
   140‚Üí  flagged as candidates. Returned with confidence scores for human review.
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí- **Tier 3 ‚Äî LLM-assisted curation** (optional, pay-per-use, long tail):
   143‚Üí  For names that Tier 1+2 can't resolve. Batches unmatched names with context
   144‚Üí  (source publication, state, ranking position, top fuzzy candidates) and sends
   145‚Üí  to Claude API for suggested matches. Suggestions presented for human approval.
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí  All approved matches (from Tier 2 or 3) are persisted as new aliases in
   148‚Üí  `search_base.institution_alias` for future deterministic matching.
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üí- **`match-review` subcommand**: Interactive review of unmatched names from the
   151‚Üí  last import run. Shows fuzzy candidates + LLM suggestions. `--approve` saves
   152‚Üí  selected matches as aliases.
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí**`internal/ranking`** ‚Äî `Opinion` type (rank/score/tier/range methods).
   155‚Üí`RankingBuilder` accumulates opinions and batch-flushes to `search_base.ranking`
   156‚Üímetadata + `search_base.annual_dataset` + `institution.rankings` JSONB. All 9
   157‚Üíranking importers use this; their own code is just parse + match + derive opinion.
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí**`internal/dbutil`** ‚Äî Connection pool with retry. `UpsertInstitution` (core
   160‚Üícolumns + JSONB merge). `MergeJSONB` (append to institution JSONB column without
   161‚Üíoverwriting other importers' data). `UpsertDataset` / `UpsertAnnualDataset`.
   162‚Üí`RefreshMaterializedViews` (called once at end of run-all).
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí### Pipeline Ordering (`run-all`)
   165‚Üí
   166‚Üí```
   167‚ÜíLayer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
   168‚ÜíLayer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
   169‚Üí                       admissions, misc/*
   170‚ÜíLayer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
   171‚ÜíFinal:                RefreshMaterializedViews
   172‚Üí```
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí## Data Sources
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí### Data Update Calendar
   177‚Üí
   178‚ÜíEach source has an "expected" date (when it typically drops) and a "by" date
   179‚Üí(99.5% confidence it's out, without being a full year later).
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí| Source | Expected Release | By Date | Notes |
   182‚Üí|--------|-----------------|---------|-------|
   183‚Üí| **College Scorecard** | Late April‚ÄìMay | Jun 30 | Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024. Oct update adds FSA data. |
   184‚Üí| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same as above | Jun 30 | Bundled with institution data release |
   185‚Üí| **US News** | Late Sep | Oct 15 | Sep 23 2025, Sep 2024. Always September. |
   186‚Üí| **Forbes** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 26 2025, Sep 7 2024. Late Aug‚Äìearly Sep. |
   187‚Üí| **Niche** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 25 2025. Consistently late August. |
   188‚Üí| **Times Higher Ed** | Early‚Äìmid Oct | Nov 1 | Oct 9 2025, Oct 10 2024. Always October. |
   189‚Üí| **QS World** | Mid Jun | Jul 15 | Jun 19 2025, Jun 4 2024. Always June. |
   190‚Üí| **Washington Monthly** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 24 2025. Published with Sep/Oct issue. |
   191‚Üí| **WSJ** | Early Sep | Oct 1 | Sep 5 2024. Fall release. |
   192‚Üí| **ARWU/Shanghai** | Mid Aug | Sep 1 | Aug 15 2025. Consistently mid-August since 2003. |
   193‚Üí| **Money** | Late Jun | Jul 31 | Jun 25 2025. Summer release. |
   194‚Üí| **Crime/Clery** | Oct 1 deadline | Nov 15 | Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
   195‚Üí| **NCAA Directory** | Aug (academic year) | Sep 30 | Updated for each academic year. |
   196‚Üí| **Partisan Lean** | Post-election | Mar (odd years) | FiveThirtyEight updates after each election cycle. |
   197‚Üí| **ZIP Coordinates** | Stable | N/A | GeoNames data; updated continuously but rarely changes. |
   198‚Üí| **Koppen Climate** | Stable | N/A | Climate classifications; updated every ~10 years. |
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí**Suggested annual import schedule:**

200‚Üí**Suggested annual import schedule:**
   201‚Üí1. **May‚ÄìJun**: College Scorecard + FOS (foundation refresh), QS, Money
   202‚Üí2. **Aug‚ÄìSep**: ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ
   203‚Üí3. **Oct‚ÄìNov**: Times Higher Ed, Crime/Clery
   204‚Üí4. **After each run**: normalizer, materialized view refresh
   205‚Üí
   206‚Üí### Programmatic Download (automatable)
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí| Source | URL / API | Format |
   209‚Üí|--------|-----------|--------|
   210‚Üí| **College Scorecard** | `https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/` ‚Äî bulk CSV download | CSV (ZIP) |
   211‚Üí| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same site, FieldOfStudyData files | CSV (ZIP) |
   212‚Üí| **Forbes Rankings** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=...` | JSON |
   213‚Üí| **Niche Rankings** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college` (109 pages, needs User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests) | JSON |
   214‚Üí| **Crime Data** | `https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list` ‚Äî downloadable XLS, convert to CSV via libreoffice | XLS‚ÜíCSV |
   215‚Üí| **Partisan Lean** | FiveThirtyEight CSV (GitHub) | CSV |
   216‚Üí| **ZIP Coordinates** | Public domain CSV (GeoNames or similar) | CSV |
   217‚Üí| **Koppen Climate** | Public domain ASCII grid | Text |
   218‚Üí| **PayScale** | `https://www.payscale.com/college-roi/page/2` ‚Äî extract from page props JSON | HTML‚ÜíJSON |
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üí### Web Scraping Required (semi-automated with docs)
   221‚Üí
   222‚Üí| Source | Website | Method | Notes |
   223‚Üí|--------|---------|--------|-------|
   224‚Üí| **ARWU/Shanghai** | `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` | Scrape HTML table or download PDF | No API. PDF at `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`. Use goquery for HTML or manual PDF download. |
   225‚Üí| **NCAA Sports** | `web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` | Scrape or manual export | NCAA Directory has member listings by division. May need browser automation or manual CSV export. |
   226‚Üí| **Money Magazine** | `money.com/best-colleges/` | Scrape HTML table | Extract `tr[data-id]` rows, parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` via goquery |
   227‚Üí| **Princeton Review** | princetonreview.com | Scrape HTML | Category-based lists (most liberal, most conservative, etc.) |
   228‚Üí| **Times Higher Ed** | `timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` | JSON API behind the page, or scrape | May need browser dev tools to find the API endpoint |
   229‚Üí| **QS World** | QS website | Previously available as TSV/CSV | Check if still downloadable or needs scraping |
   230‚Üí| **Washington Monthly** | washingtonmonthly.com | JSON or scrape | Check current availability |
   231‚Üí| **WSJ Rankings** | WSJ website | JSON embedded in page | `{ colleges: [...] }` structure |
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí### Pre-curated / Aggregated Sources
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üí| Source | Location | Notes |
   236‚Üí|--------|----------|-------|
   237‚Üí| **US News Historical** | [frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings](https://github.com/frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings) | CSV archive 1984‚Äì2024. Free for public use. |
   238‚Üí| **Multi-ranking aggregate** | [frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data](https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data) | SAT, acceptance rate, US News/THE/QS/CSRankings |
   239‚Üí| **AAU Membership** | Embedded list (~71 institutions) | Stable; update when new members added |
   240‚Üí| **Common App / Coalition / etc.** | Embedded lists | Update annually from organization websites |
   241‚Üí
   242‚Üí### Approach for Non-Downloadable Sources
   243‚Üí
   244‚ÜíFor sources that require scraping, the importer will:
   245‚Üí1. **Try automated download first** (known API/URL patterns)
   246‚Üí2. **Fall back to cached local file** if download fails
   247‚Üí3. **Document manual steps** in `DATA_SOURCES.md` with exact commands
   248‚Üí4. **Accept `--file` flag** so user can provide manually-obtained data
   249‚Üí
   250‚ÜíFor scrapers specifically:
   251‚Üí- Use `net/http` + `github.com/PuerkitoBio/goquery` for HTML table extraction
   252‚Üí- Use `chromedp` only if JavaScript rendering is required
   253‚Üí- Keep scraping code isolated in each importer package for easy updates
   254‚Üí
   255‚Üí## Key Files to Reference
   256‚Üí
   257‚Üí- `tools/samplesql/main.go` ‚Äî CLI pattern, flag parsing, DB connection
   258‚Üí- `tools/samplesql/extractor.go` ‚Äî pgx query patterns, column detection
   259‚Üí- `api/internal/db/db.go` ‚Äî Connection with retry pattern
   260‚Üí- `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql` ‚Äî Complete target schema
   261‚Üí- `api/internal/search/registry.go` ‚Äî JSONB paths the API queries (consumer contract)

   263‚Üí## Issue Breakdown
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üí### Epic: Data Import Tool (`tools/importer`)
   266‚Üí
   267‚Üí---
   268‚Üí
   269‚Üí#### 1. Scaffold importer module
   270‚Üí- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   271‚Üí- **Deps**: none
   272‚Üí- **Description**: Create `tools/importer/` Go module with `go.mod`
   273‚Üí  (`github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/importer`), `main.go` with subcommand
   274‚Üí  dispatch via stdlib `flag`, `internal/config/config.go` (DATABASE_URL,
   275‚Üí  CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN flags + env fallback). Include `Importer`
   276‚Üí  interface definition in `registry.go`. Compile and run `kapow-import --help`.
   277‚Üí- **Done when**: `go build ./...` succeeds, `kapow-import --help` prints usage.
   278‚Üí
   279‚Üí#### 2. Implement internal/dbutil
   280‚Üí- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   281‚Üí- **Deps**: 1
   282‚Üí- **Description**: `internal/dbutil/` package: `connect.go` (pgxpool with retry,
   283‚Üí  reuse pattern from `api/internal/db/db.go`), `upsert.go` (UpsertInstitution
   284‚Üí  with JSONB merge via `COALESCE(col,'{}'::jsonb) || $N`, UpsertDataset,
   285‚Üí  UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB), `batch.go` (CopyFrom wrapper, BatchExec
   286‚Üí  using pgx.Batch), `views.go` (RefreshMaterializedViews for dataset_merged +
   287‚Üí  dataset_latest). Include Institution/Dataset/AnnualDataset Go structs.
   288‚Üí  Integration tests against dev DB.
   289‚Üí- **Done when**: Integration test creates/updates an institution + dataset + refreshes views.
   290‚Üí
   291‚Üí#### 3. Implement internal/fetch
   292‚Üí- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   293‚Üí- **Deps**: 1
   294‚Üí- **Description**: `internal/fetch/` package: HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
   295‚Üí  conditional requests, cache keyed by SHA-256 of URL under `~/.cache/kapow-import/`,
   296‚Üí  transparent .zip/.gz/.xz decompression via `GetReader()`, `--file` local
   297‚Üí  override bypass, stale-cache-on-failure resilience. Unit tests with
   298‚Üí  `httptest.Server`.
   299‚Üí- **Done when**: Unit tests pass for cache hit, miss, conditional 304, decompression, local fallback.
   300‚Üí
   301‚Üí#### 4. Implement internal/csvutil
   302‚Üí- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   303‚Üí- **Deps**: 1
   304‚Üí- **Description**: `internal/csvutil/` package: header-mapped CSV/TSV reader

   306‚Üí  `WithLazyQuotes`. `Row` type with `String(col)`, `Int(col)`, `Float(col)`,
   307‚Üí  `OptionalString(col)`, `OptionalInt(col)`, `OptionalFloat(col)`. Handle
   308‚Üí  missing columns gracefully (return zero/nil). Unit tests with embedded
   309‚Üí  test CSV data.
   310‚Üí- **Done when**: Unit tests cover normal parsing, TSV, missing columns, type coercion, quoted values.
   311‚Üí
   312‚Üí#### 5. Implement internal/namematch (Tier 1 ‚Äî deterministic)
   313‚Üí- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   314‚Üí- **Deps**: 2
   315‚Üí- **Description**: `internal/namematch/` package. `matcher.go`: Load all

   317‚Üí  lowercased name ‚Üí normalized name ‚Üí alias. Also `MatchByOPE6`/`MatchByOPE8`.
   318‚Üí  `normalize.go`: lowercase, strip diacritics (x/text/transform), remove "The ",
   319‚Üí  strip punctuation, collapse whitespace. `aliases.go`: ~1000 hardcoded alias
   320‚Üí  overrides for institutions whose external names differ from NCES canonical
   321‚Üí  names (e.g. "Arizona State University" ‚Üí "Arizona State University - Tempe").
   322‚Üí  Unit tests for normalization; integration test for full matcher load + lookup.
   323‚Üí- **Done when**: Unit tests pass; integration test resolves known aliases ‚Üí correct unit_id.
   324‚Üí
   325‚Üí#### 6. Implement NCES College Scorecard importer
   326‚Üí- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P0
   327‚Üí- **Deps**: 2, 3, 4

   329‚Üí  `collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/`. `fields.go`: maps 200+ CSV columns to JSONB
   330‚Üí  keys (demographics, test scores, costs, admissions, programs). `nces.go`:
   331‚Üí  parses CSV, filters (skip non-degree-granting, graduate-only, missing OPE IDs),
   332‚Üí  handles OPE6/OPE8 deduplication (prefer lower unit_id), inserts states into
   333‚Üí  `location.state`, creates institutions with coordinates + nces JSONB, creates
   334‚Üí  CORE and ACADEMICS datasets. Unit tests with 10-row fixture CSV; integration
   335‚Üí  test against dev DB.
   336‚Üí- **Done when**: Integration test imports fixture ‚Üí institutions + datasets exist in DB with correct JSONB.
   337‚Üí

   342‚Üí  category slugs, method rank/score/tier/range, year, unit_id, rank, score, tier,
   343‚Üí  rank range). `Builder` accumulates opinions, `Flush(ctx)` batch-writes:
   344‚Üí  upsert ranking metadata into `search_base.ranking`, upsert into

   346‚Üí  `institution.rankings` JSONB. Implement `deriveOpinion()` to parse numeric
   347‚Üí  ranks, tier strings ("Tier 1"), and ranges ("201-300"). Unit tests for opinion
   348‚Üí  derivation; integration test for flush.
   349‚Üí- **Done when**: Integration test flushes 10 opinions ‚Üí ranking + annual_dataset + institution.rankings populated.
   350‚Üí
